theme: Upgrade links so all flows share fairly
title: Fair-Share Capacity Market
strategies: trivial: replicate the checker's own reference purchase (spend budget in dollars proportional to each link's EXISTING capacity -- 'upgrade the biggest trunk first')
greedy: rank links by raw flows-crossing-it per dollar, dump the ENTIRE budget on the single top-ranked link (the textbook 'fix the busiest link / global min-cut' instinct)

strong: iterative marginal-value water-filling -- repeatedly re-solve the full proportional-fair dual equilibrium to get each link's true shadow price (the envelope-theorem marginal utility of its capacity), and spend each budget increment where price/cost is currently highest, capturing the nonlinear coupling greedy ignores
invalid: dump an astronomically large purchase on every link, blowing through the budget constraint
